description: Inputs crafted to exploit JSON.parse reviver callbacks. A JSON.parse(input, reviver)-based implementation allows a reviver function to transform values during parsing, which can be hijacked. The atomic emit has no JSON.parse call and therefore has no reviver surface to exploit — refusal is structural.

inputs.label: proto-in-json-string
inputs.rationale: String elements where a JSON.parse+reviver implementation might leak prototype properties through value transformation; atomic emit refuses on first string element

inputs.label: toJSON-key
inputs.rationale: String element; atomic emit refuses on non-integer content

inputs.label: valueOf-key
inputs.rationale: String element targeting Object.prototype.valueOf; refuses as non-integer

inputs.label: mixed-keys-and-ints
inputs.rationale: Mixed list; second element is a string targeting prototype method; refused on second element

inputs.label: boolean-elements
inputs.rationale: Boolean elements — not integers; 't' in 'true' triggers SyntaxError

inputs.label: symbol-like-string
inputs.rationale: String targeting Symbol.iterator; first char '"' triggers SyntaxError

inputs.label: object-with-toJSON
inputs.rationale: Object element with toJSON property; '{' triggers SyntaxError

inputs.label: nested-reviver-vector
inputs.rationale: Classic reviver pollution pattern; '{' at position 0 triggers immediate SyntaxError

inputs.label: json-replacer-array
inputs.rationale: String elements naming JSON hooks; SyntaxError on first '"'

inputs.label: function-string-element
inputs.rationale: String element that looks like a function literal; SyntaxError on '"'
